summary: Composes 5th-grade ELA assessment items (Smarter Balanced · Grade 5 · Claim 1 · Reasoning & Evidence) for multiple learning targets selected by a top-level `target`: c1-t4 (literary, RL) and c1-t11 (informational, RI). Item-first: each authored question pins its correct answer (focus) and its stem, and the compiler draws that question's distractors from the foils that target it.
in_scope: A required top-level target: c1-t4 (literary) or c1-t11 (informational), selecting the dimension/standard/stem vocabulary
A single passage with numbered lines
Questions (outcomes) authored first, each with a unique id, a focus correct claim, and a stem from the target's guideline catalog; distractors bound to questions via targets
An inference graph: supported and distractor claims, plus evidence sources tagged directly-supports / supports-wrong-claim / irrelevant
c1-t4 dimensions: character, setting, event, point-of-view, theme, topic, narrators-feelings, character-relationship
c1-t11 dimensions: relationships-interactions, author-use-of-information, point-of-view, purpose, authors-opinion
Three task models: ebsr (two-part selected response), hot-text (select text), short-text (constructed response)
Authored, error-typed distractors (misreads-detail / erroneous-inference / faulty-reasoning; supports-wrong-claim / irrelevant) with rationales
Standards rl-1/rl-3/rl-6/rl-9 (c1-t4) and ri-1/ri-3/ri-6/ri-7/ri-8/ri-9 (c1-t11); DOK r-dok3

A reading-level target set by the guideline/target's grade (Grade 5 for c1-t4/c1-t11), overridable by an optional top-level `grade`; the compiler estimates the passage's reading level and warns when it runs above the target grade
out_of_scope: Claims, grades, or targets beyond G5 · Claim 1 · Reasoning & Evidence (T4 & T11) — those belong in their own dialects
Dual-text stimuli (a single passage only in this version)
Compile-time LLM generation — the compiler selects and validates authored content, it does not invent it
Auto-scoring of short-text responses — they are hand-scored against the emitted rubric
Cross-language composition via import — each item runs in exactly one dialect
